mission.title: harbor: Support for Redis TLS
mission.description: Support for Redis TLS. This issue affects 38+ users.

steps.title: Identify harbor feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your harbor deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=harbor
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support for Redis TLS

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant harbor resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
As a Harbor instance operator, i want to secure the traffic to Redis instance.

This issue might seem not so pressing when internal Redis is used. However, external managed Redis instances can be used

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for Redis TLS
steps.description:
Add HA Harbor deployment, utilizing Postgres and Redis operators for external databases. Images and charts are stored in the Swift object storage. Installation steps are also added as well as instructions on how Fluxcd can be used.
```yaml
external:
    # support redis, redis+sentinel
    # addr for redis: <host_redis>:<port_redis>
    # addr for redis+sentinel: <host_sentinel1>:<port_sentinel1>,<host_sentinel2>:<port_sentinel2>,<host_sentinel3>:<port_sentinel3>
    addr: "192.168.0.2:6379"
    # The name of the set of Redis instances to monitor, it must be set to support redis+sentinel
    sentinelMasterSet: ""
    # The "coreDatabaseIndex" must be "0" as the library Harbor
    # used doesn't support configuring it
    # harborDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "6", this config is optional
    
```

steps.title: Confirm Support for Redis TLS is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=harbor -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: Add HA Harbor deployment, utilizing Postgres and Redis operators for external databases. Images and charts are stored in the Swift object storage. Installation steps are also added as well as instructions on how Fluxcd can be used.. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
external:
    # support redis, redis+sentinel
    # addr for redis: <host_redis>:<port_redis>
    # addr for redis+sentinel: <host_sentinel1>:<port_sentinel1>,<host_sentinel2>:<port_sentinel2>,<host_sentinel3>:<port_sentinel3>
    addr: "192.168.0.2:6379"
    # The name of the set of Redis instances to monitor, it must be set to support redis+sentinel
    sentinelMasterSet: ""
    # The "coreDatabaseIndex" must be "0" as the library Harbor
    # used doesn't support configuring it
    # harborDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "6", this config is optional
    # cacheLayerDatabaseIndex defaults to "0", but it can be configured to "7", this config is optional
    coreDatabaseIndex: "0"
    jobserviceDatabaseIndex: "1"
    registryDatabaseIndex: "2"
    t

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with harbor installed or the issue environment reproducible.
